perturbation.original_text: “Purpose” shall mean the purpose referred to in recital A. “Confidential Information” shall mean all information in whatever form, whether imparted orally or in writing or by other medium including all copies of the same which one party hereto discloses to the other pursuant to the Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: “Purpose” shall mean the purpose referred to in recital A. “Confidential Information” shall mean all information in whatever form, whether imparted orally or in writing or by other medium including all copies of the same which one party hereto discloses to the other pursuant to the Purpose. Unless, that information has a copyright on it.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by adding an exception to the definition of 'Confidential Information.' While the original definition includes all information disclosed pursuant to the 'Purpose,' the modified definition excludes copyrighted material. This contradicts sections that assume all disclosed information is confidential, creating uncertainty about the status of copyrighted material shared between parties. Copyrighted material will no longer be subject to restrictions of agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: USE OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION 2.1. A Receiving Party agrees: 2.1.1. to keep the Confidential Information of the other strictly confidential and not copy, supply or make the same available to any person other than as permitted in Clauses 2.1.2 and 2.1.3 below; 2.1.2. to use the Confidential Information of the other solely in, and to the extent necessary for the Purpose and not to copy or use any Confidential Information of the other save to the extent necessary for the Purpose; 2.1.3. not to disclose any Confidential Information to any person or entity other than to such of its employees who need to know for the Purpose (“Permitted Disclosees”), provided that the Receiving Party shall ensure that its Permitted Disclosees keep all Confid ential Information confidential and only use it on the terms of this Agreement, as if such Permitted Disclosees were party to this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Confidential Information' to exclude copyrighted material, creating a direct conflict with the 'USE OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' section. The contradicted text requires all Confidential Information to be kept strictly confidential, used solely for the Purpose, and not disclosed to unauthorized persons. By excluding copyrighted material from the definition of Confidential Information, the changed text implies that such material is not subject to these restrictions, directly contradicting the obligations outlined in the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: Each party shall forthwith upon request by the other return to the other all Confidential Information (including, without limitation, all summaries, copies and excerpts of Confidential Information), and other information, given to it by the oth er and shall expunge and destroy all Confidential Information from any equipment or other device in its possession or control. Furthermore, the receiving party shall destroy all copies of analyses, compilations, studies or other documents or derivative wor ks which contain any Confidential Information. Each party shall on request provide a letter signed by a director confirming that it has complied with this clause.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party shall forthwith upon request by the other return to the other all Confidential Information (including, without limitation, all summaries, copies and excerpts of Confidential Information), and other information, given to it by the oth er and shall expunge and destroy all Confidential Information from any equipment or other device in its possession or control. Furthermore, the receiving party is not required to destroy all copies of analyses, compilations, studies or other documents or derivative wor ks which contain any Confidential Information. Each party shall on request provide a letter signed by a director confirming that it has complied with this clause.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating that the receiving party is not required to destroy copies of analyses, compilations, studies, or other documents or derivative works containing Confidential Information. This contradicts the preceding sentence, which mandates the return and destruction of all Confidential Information, including summaries, copies, and excerpts. The enforcement of this clause becomes uncertain due to the direct in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party shall forthwith upon request by the other return to the other all Confidential Information (including, without limitation, all summaries, copies and excerpts of Confidential Information), and other information, given to it by the oth er and shall expunge and destroy all Confidential Information from any equipment or other device in its possession or control. Furthermore, the receiving party is not required to destroy all copies of analyses, compilations, studies or other documents or derivative wor ks which contain any Confidential Information. Each party shall on request provide a letter signed by a director confirming that it has complied with this clause.
perturbation.justification: The Changed Text directly contradicts itself. The first sentence states that all Confidential Information, including summaries, copies and excerpts, must be returned and destroyed. The second sentence explicitly states that the receiving party is not required to destroy copies of analyses, compilations, studies or other documents or derivative works which contain any Confidential Information. This creates a clear in-text contradiction within the clause itself, making it unclear whether such documents must be destroyed or not. This is exactly what the explanation says and adheres to the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8.3. (Notwithstanding Clause 8.2) each party shall indemnify and keep indemnified the other party against any and all liabilities, claims, expenses, lo sses and damages suffered or incurred by that other party as a result of any breach by it of its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.3. (Notwithstanding Clause 8.2) each party shall indemnify and keep indemnified the other party against any and all liabilities, claims, expenses, lo sses and damages suffered or incurred by that other party as a result of any breach by it of its obligations under this Agreement. Except for liabilities above 1 million pounds.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by adding a monetary limitation to the indemnification clause. While the original clause states that each party shall indemnify the other against any and all liabilities, the modified clause adds an exception for liabilities exceeding 1 million pounds. This contradicts the 'any and all' wording and creates uncertainty regarding the extent of indemnification obligations. The change affects section 8.3 which states 'each party shall indemnify and keep indemnified the other party against any and all liabilities', but it no longer is.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (Notwithstanding Clause 8.2) each party shall indemnify and keep indemnified the other party against any and all liabilities, claims, expenses, lo sses and damages suffered or incurred by that other party as a result of any breach by it of its obligations under this Agreement. Except for liabilities above 1 million pounds.
perturbation.justification: The original text of section 8.3 states that each party shall indemnify the other against 'any and all liabilities'. The changed text adds an exception for liabilities above 1 million pounds. This directly contradicts the 'any and all' wording by introducing a monetary limitation, thereby creating an inconsistency as different sections of the contract now provide conflicting obligations. The contradicted text also includes the monetary limitation of liabilities above 1 million pounds, the change has introduced a contradiction to the original statement.

perturbation.original_text: 9.4. This Agreement shall apply without limit of time to all Confidential Information disclosed in connection with the Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.4. This Agreement shall apply without limit of time to all Confidential Information disclosed in connection with the Purpose. This agreement shall last no longer than 1 year.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by limiting the agreement's duration to one year, while the original clause states that the agreement applies without limit of time. This contradiction creates uncertainty as to whether the confidentiality obligations persist indefinitely or expire after one year. This directly affects the applicability of the entire agreement after the 1-year mark, due to conflicting terms of duration.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall apply without limit of time to all Confidential Information disclosed in connection with the Purpose. This agreement shall last no longer than 1 year.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states the agreement applies 'without limit of time', while the changed text adds 'This agreement shall last no longer than 1 year'. This directly contradicts the original clause and creates uncertainty about the duration of the agreement. The Contradicted Text and Changed Text are the same in this case. The explanation given also indicates the contradiction that is formed.

perturbation.original_text: 2.1.2. to use the Confidential Information of the other solely in, and to the extent necessary for the Purpose and not to copy or use any Confidential Information of the other save to the extent necessary for the Purpose;
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1.2. to use the Confidential Information of the other however deemed fit, and to the extent necessary for the Purpose and to copy or use any Confidential Information of the other save to the extent necessary for the Purpose;
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating the confidential information of the other is to be used however deemed fit, while stating that it can only be used to the extent necessary for the Purpose. The enforcement of the original clause becomes uncertain due to the direct in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: to use the Confidential Information of the other however deemed fit, and to the extent necessary for the Purpose and to copy or use any Confidential Information of the other save to the extent necessary for the Purpose;
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text limits the use of Confidential Information, while the changed text allows it to be used 'however deemed fit' while also stating it is limited 'to the extent necessary for the Purpose'. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, therefore a contradiction exists since the information can be used 'however deemed fit' regardless of the extent necessary for the Purpose.

perturbation.original_text: 8.2. Without prejudice to any other rights or remedies which the other party may have (whether or not expressly provided or contemplated in this Agreement), each party acknowledges and agrees that damages may not be an adequate remedy for breach by it of the pr ovisions of this Agreement, and accordingly it agrees that (subject, where applicable, to the discretion of the court) the other party shall be entitled to the remedies of injunction, specific performance and any other equitable relief for any threatened o r actual breach of such provisions and no proof of special damages shall be necessary for its enforcement.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.2. Without prejudice to any other rights or remedies which the other party may have (whether or not expressly provided or contemplated in this Agreement), each party acknowledges and agrees that damages may not be an adequate remedy for breach by it of the pr ovisions of this Agreement, and accordingly it agrees that (subject, where applicable, to the discretion of the court) the other party shall be entitled to the remedies of injunction, specific performance and any other equitable relief for any threatened o r actual breach of such provisions and no proof of special damages shall be necessary for its enforcement. Damages will be the only remedy.
perturbation.explanation: The modification introduces an in-text contradiction by stipulating that 'Damages will be the only remedy' while the original clause outlines alternative remedies like injunction and specific performance. This directly contradicts the preceding statement about equitable relief, rendering the clause internally inconsistent and uncertain in its enforcement. The contradicted text, within the same section (8.2), explicitly acknowledges the possibility of remedies beyond damages, creating a clear conflict with the new limitation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without prejudice to any other rights or remedies which the other party may have (whether or not expressly provided or contemplated in this Agreement), each party acknowledges and agrees that damages may not be an adequate remedy for breach by it of the pr ovisions of this Agreement, and accordingly it agrees that (subject, where applicable, to the discretion of the court) the other party shall be entitled to the remedies of injunction, specific performance and any other equitable relief for any threatened o r actual breach of such provisions and no proof of special damages shall be necessary for its enforcement. Damages will be the only remedy.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'Damages will be the only remedy' to a clause that previously stated the parties are entitled to 'injunction, specific performance and any other equitable relief'. This creates a direct contradiction because equitable relief includes remedies other than damages, which are now explicitly excluded. The contradicted text is within the same paragraph, further emphasizing the in-text nature of the contradiction.
